items.title: Slovenia election sees ruling party tied with opposition
items.description: Prime Minister Robert Golob's governing liberal Freedom Movement of Slovenia was almost perfectly tied with Janez Jansa and his right-wing SDS party, with 99% of votes counted.

items.title: Madrid protest against US blockade of Cuba
items.description: Protesters gathered outside the US embassy in Madrid as sanctions pushed Cuba into an electricity blackout.

items.title: U.S. Judge for now dismisses lawsuit by Sam Altman’s sister accusing OpenAI CEO of sexual abuse
items.description: Sam Altman’s ​defamation counterclaim stemmed from posts between ‌2021 and 2024 on social media, including X and TikTok, where his sister referred to alleged abuse

items.title: A pharmacist and a homesick lifestyle blogger: The 'alarming' civilian cost of war in Iran
items.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

items.title: France's Socialists hold on to power in major cities in election boost for mainstream
items.description: Paris and Marseille are held by the Socialists in local elections which saw the nationalist right win in Nice and certain towns.

items.title: European far-right leaders gather in Budapest ahead of Hungarian elections
items.description: Far-right leaders of Europe are gathering in Budapest for the so-called European Parliament group Patriots' grand assembly. The gathering is not only a show of force for Europe's populists: it's also a show of support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, ahead of national elections on April 12. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more from Budapest.

items.title: Farmers sound alarm over imminent food price shocks
items.description: Australia's peak farming body is warning consumers the price of groceries is about to jump as producers and the freight industry grapple with diesel price hikes and supply issues.

items.title: 'Dignity restored': Remains of 63 Khoisan people reburied in South Africa
items.description: South Africa on Monday reburied the remains of 63 Khoisan people, among southern Africa's oldest indigenous communities, some of whose bodies were shipped to European museums over a century ago.

items.title: How Israel is reacting to Trump’s  ‘de-escalation’ with Iran
items.description: There is expected to be pushback from Israel following President Donald Trump’s move towards de-escalation with Iran.

items.title: London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze in suspected hate crime, police say
items.description: ‌The ⁠arson attack is being treated as ⁠an antisemitic hate crime, according to officials.

items.title: Colombian military plane with 125 on board crashes after takeoff
items.description: Air Force says plane was carrying 114 passengers and 11 crew ⁠members, and authorities are investigating cause of crash.

items.title: Trump says the U.S. is in talks with Iran to end the war, which Iran denies
items.description: Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.

items.title: US signs nearly $1 billion deal with France's TotalEnergies to end offshore wind projects
items.description: The United States and TotalEnergies signed an agreement on Monday to end the French company's offshore US wind farm projects and redirect those funds towards fossil fuel production, with the US interior secretary saying the deal was worth nearly $1 billion.

items.title: Germany news: CDU wins Rhineland-Palatinate election
items.description: Provisional results show that Friedrich Merz's conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) has won the elections in Rhineland-Palatinate in western Germany.

items.title: India news: We can withstand Middle East turmoil, Modi tells parliament
items.description: PM Narendra Modi said the Iran war is straining supply chains and trade routes, but stressed India's economic fundamentals remain strong.

items.title: Niger civil groups condemn EU call to free ex-president Bazoum
items.description: Civil society organisations in Niger have condemned a European Union resolution demanding the release of the country’s former president. They're calling for a simultaneous mobilisation in Niger, Burkina Faso and Mali to protest the move.

items.title: US-Israeli war on Iran strains food, water and fuel prices in India
items.description: Locals in northern India have a growing concern over costs of essential resources due to the Iran war.

items.title: 'Like the plane got cut in half': Eyewitnesses describe chaos of LaGuardia crash
items.description: Eyewitnesses have described the moment Air Canada flight AC8646 crashed into a fire truck on the runway of New York's LaGuardia airport.

items.title: France's former Socialist Prime Minister Lionel Jospin dies at 88
items.description: Jospin was head of government from 1997 to 2002 and is credited with bringing in the 35-hour working week.

items.title: Watch: NATO chief: 22-nation group forming to secure Strait of Hormuz
US needs more energy development to power AI, Google president says
Trump deploys ICE agents to US airports amid staffing issues, delays
items.description: Democrats and other observers slam deployment of US immigration agents to airports as dangerous and source of tension.

items.title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May 1
items.description: EU authorities say sweeping free-trade agreement with South America's Mercosur bloc will provisionally enter into force on May 1; Pending court ruling could still throw spanner in the works. Leer más

items.title: Hedge funds bet against US stocks and turn to Europe, Goldman Sachs says
‘Stop, stop, stop’: Moment Air Canada plane crashes into truck
items.description: CCTV video captured the moment an Air Canada plane collided with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: Iran threatens to attack West Asia electrical plants powering U.S. bases
items.description: The statement from Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard marks the latest attempt by Tehran to try and explain its attacks on the Gulf Arab countries

items.title: Trump and Iran step up threats over energy targets as war escalates
items.description: If Iran's fuel and energy infrastructure is violated by the enemy, all energy, information technology and desalination infrastructure belonging to the U.S. and the regime in the region will be targeted, says Iranian military's operational command Khatam Al-Anbiya

items.title: World could face worst energy crisis in decades, IEA chief warns
items.description: “As of today, we lost 11 million barrels per day, so more than two major oil shocks put together,” Fatih Birol said

items.title: Insurer AIA shares dip on Asian economic worries, funding cost concerns
Trump postpones Iran strikes; says deal is possible
items.description: Trump’s move followed a threat by Iran ​to attack ‌Israel’s power plants and those supplying U.S. bases across the ‌Gulf region if the ​U.S. targets Iran’s power network

items.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
items.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.

items.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
items.description: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.

items.title: China-North Korea transit relaunch points to better ties
items.description: Passenger air and rail links are being restored between China and its North Korean ally as geopolitical upheavals add new impetus to improving the relationship between Pyongyang and Beijing.

items.title: HSBC appoints first chief AI officer as it seeks cost cuts
How Africa gets shortchanged in trade with Europe
items.description: The establishment of free trade between much of both continents for half a century has created growth in many areas. However, the data analysis shows that Africa remains on unequal footing.

items.title: Flash floods in Kenya have killed at least 81 and displace thousands
items.description: Torrential rains and flash floods have killed at least 81 people across Kenya this month, authorities said, as heavy downpours continue. In Kisumu County, entire villages have been submerged, with about 1,200 hectares of farmland destroyed and crops swept away.

items.title: US weight-loss drugmakers slash prices in fight to win customers
items.description: Weight-loss drug prices are falling in the US - but can the example be repeated?

items.title: Iran-Israel war: A day‑by‑day rundown of the escalating crisis
items.description: A joint U.S.-Israel attack on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have plunged West Asia into a fast‑spreading war, with Tehran and its allies launching retaliatory strikes across Israel, the Gulf and U.S. bases.

items.title: Who is Emmanuel Grégoire, Paris's next mayor?
items.description: With more than 50% of the vote, Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire was elected as the next mayor of Paris.FRANCE 24 takes a closer look at the political career of the 48-year-old, who was seen as incumbent Mayor Anne Hidalgo's right hand man until a public falling out in 2022.

items.title: Nigerians in Lagos grapple with rising pump prices
items.description: Nigerians in Lagos are feeling the impact of soaring fuel prices, as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East disrupts global oil supplies and drives up the cost of living.

items.title: Lebanon : Israel strikes key bridge, stoking fears of ground invasion
items.description: Israeli forces destroyed bridges they said were used by Hezbollah. The attack has sparked concern that a large-scale Israeli ground invasion could be in the works, with the Lebanese president calling the bombing 'collective punishment'. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from the ground.

items.title: Trump signals off-ramp in Iran war despite no ‘regime change’
items.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the US military to postpone strikes against Iranian power plants and energy.

items.title: New UN climate report says the past decade was the hottest on record
items.description: A new UN report confirms the past decade was the hottest on record.

items.title: Apple to hold annual developers conference from June 8
Britain sees no evidence that Iran is targeting Europe with missiles
items.description: ‌Israel Defence Forces posted on ‌social media ⁠that Iran has missiles “that ⁠can reach London, Paris or Berlin”

items.title: Dangote refinery says begun fuel exports in Africa
items.description: Nigeria's Dangote mega-refinery says it has begun exporting fuel to other African countries, after global oil prices soared due to the Middle East war.

items.title: As Gor makes first trip as U.S. Special envoy to South Asia, Delhi watches strategic signals closely
items.description: United States Ambassador to India Sergio Gor’s visit to Colombo and Maldives expected to strengthen U.S. regional role, all eyes on future trips to Pakistan-Afghanistan

items.title: Milei says Argentina can 'guarantee Europe's energy security' during CPAC speech in Hungary
items.description: President says Argentina “is in a position to guarantee Europe's energy security" during visit to Hungary for CPAC conference; Milei backs PM Orban’s anti-immigration stance, stating: “When they do not culturally adapt, it becomes an invasion.” Leer más

items.title: Netballer explains absence after being separated from daughter at birth
items.description: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

items.title: Cuban Germans react to Trump's threats with hope and fear
items.description: Cubans came to train, work and study in former communist East Germany. A few thousand people of Cuban origin live in Germany today. Amid US takeover threats and worsening conditions, they worry about Cuba's future.

items.title: France: Former PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88
items.description: Jospin proved the canary in the coal mine for the far-right rise in France. He was the first center-left presidential candidate to finish behind Jean-Marie Le Pen but rallied his supporters to his rival, Jacques Chirac.

items.title: Tencent integrates WeChat with OpenClaw AI agent amid China tech battle
items.description: The software, called ClawBot, will appear as a contact within ‌WeChat, allowing users of China’s most popular app with over ‌1 billion monthly active users to access it

items.title: EU restricts Hungary's access to classified intelligence over alleged leaks to Russia
items.description: As European far-right leaders gather around Hungarian PM Viktor Orban, the EU has restricted classified intelligence to Hungary following allegations that the Hungarian foreign minister leaked EU Council details to his Russian counterpart. Since this has come out, several European leaders have said they had suspected this had been the case for some time. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley looks into these revelations, and what that could mean for the upcoming Hungarian elections.

items.title: Presidents of Kenya and Uganda launch next phase of cross-border railway
items.description: Kenya and Uganda take a major step towards regional integration, launching a new phase of the Standard Gauge Railway aimed at linking East Africa to the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.title: AI may boost euro area productivity growth by 4% in 10 years, ECB says
Iran denies any talks with US after Trump claims ‘productive’ discussions
items.description: Iran parliament speaker says US president using idea of talks to 'escape quagmire in which US and Israel are trapped'.

items.title: Which countries have strategic oil reserves – and how much?
items.description: Members of the International Energy Agency have agreed to release 400 million barrels of oil from strategic reserves.

items.title: Gold dives to four-month low as inflation pressures lift rate hike bets
'Growing mistrust and disagreement' over Trump declaring war with Iran
items.description: In a surprise U-turn, US President Donald Trump has extended a deadline he had given to Iran over the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, adding five days to the initial 48-hour window. He had threatened to strike Iranian power plants were the Strait not to be reopened. For FRANCE 24's Philip Turle, it could be partly explained by the 'growing mistrust and disagreement' over his decision to start a war with Iran.

items.title: Asian markets tumble, but Europe, US rally on Trump optimism
items.description: Markets in Asia nosedived as trading restarted on Monday. Europe's indices were headed in the same direction, until optimistic Iran comments from Donald Trump prompted traders in Europe and the US to start buying again.

items.title: After strong showing in France's major cities, will left even qualify for 2027 presidential runoff?
items.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Renaud Foucart, Economist, Senior Lecturer at Lancaster University. He observes a political landscape shaped less by ideology alone and more by geography and fragmentation. What these municipal elections reveal is not a clear national trajectory, but a mosaic of local preferences. The National Rally, while dominant in national polling, remains territorially constrained, while the left finds itself caught in a structural paradox. To reach the second round of a presidential election, it must unify, without alienating the moderate electorate.

items.title: Oil falls over 13% on Trump postponing military strikes on Iran energy infrastructure
UK police investigates arson attack against Jewish charity ambulances
items.description: The UK police are looking for three suspects believed responsible for an overnight attack on ambulances belonging to a Jewish volunteer charity in London. No injuries were reported, but it is currently being treated as an antisemitic crime. The investigation is being led by the counter-terror officers, although it is not treated as a terrorist attack at this stage. Authorities say they are also looking into claims of responsibility made by a group online.

items.title: Italy's Meloni concedes defeat on judiciary referendum
items.description: With Italian voters rejecting the judicial reform pushed by Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, the far-right leader lamented the "lost chance" to modernize the country.

items.title: Italy divided over Meloni government, referendum exit polls show
items.description: Polls have closed in Italy's two-day referendum. According to exit polls, the "No" vote is slightly ahead - but the final results could differ, as they do not take into account what Italians from abroad voted. What can be said though, is that the country 'is very divided' over the county's leadership, says FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta from Rome.

items.title: National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week
items.description: Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade.

items.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
items.description: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

items.title: Brazil’s Lula slams ‘interference’ in previously colonised countries, without naming Trump
items.description: Brazil’s President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has slammed what he calls a renewed colonial push against developing nations at a summit in Colombia

items.title: Pakistan vows to ‘eradicate terrorism’ as Afghan truce nears end
items.description: Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar indicated that the country's approach to the conflict had not changed

items.title: Lula slams ‘colonial mindset’ and UN failures at summit in Colombia
items.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Saturday accused major powers of reviving a colonial approach toward developing nations, warning against interference in national sovereignty.

items.title: Israel destroys another bridge linking south Lebanon to rest of country
items.description: As it engages in heated battles with pro-Iran militant group Hezbollah in a number of Lebanese border villages, Israel has bombed another bridge across a river dividing the country's north and south. It adds to widespread fears in Lebanon that Israel intends to depopulate the southern region in the long-term as a million people have been displaced by the fighting. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin tells us more from Beirut.

items.title: Alphabet's Wing to start drone delivery in San Francisco Bay Area
As conflicts intensify in Sudan and South Sudan, fleeing civilians are caught within closed borders
items.description: While the conflict between SAF and RSF in Sudan have only intensified, South Sudan is on the verge of civil war with the government forces and the opposition forces fighting for control. In both countries, the conflicts have now become a regional emergency marked by mass killings, fragmentation of territory, and international complicity.

items.title: Trump says ICE agents will assist airport security as DHS shutdown continues
items.description: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.

items.title: HK police can now demand phone passwords under new national security rules
items.description: Those who do not comply can be jailed for up to a year and fined up to HK$100,000 ($12,700; £9,600).

items.title: Nebius says "well-funded" for AI race after closing $4.3 billion debt raise
Watch: Why is the assassination of Congo’s first PM Patrice Lumumba being talked about now?
items.description: For more than six decades after the assassination of Patrice Lumumba, there was silence. He was shot. His body was dismembered and dissolved in sulphuric acid. No one was held accountable. Now, that silence may finally be breaking. The attention is now on 93 year old Belgian diplomat Étienne Davignon who will stand trial

items.title: Colombia military plane crashes while transporting troops
items.description: The Hercules C-130 aircraft crashed as it was taking off in Colombia's southern Amazon region, bordering Peru. Around 77 survived the crash.

items.title: Efforts to shore up energy supplies are not enough, Japan says
Two dead after Air Canada jet collides with fire truck at New York airport
items.description: The pilot and copilot were killed after the plane struck the fire truck at a speed of about 39 kilometres per hour.

items.title: TACO Trump strikes again with sudden Iran backdown
items.description: After threatening to "obliterate" Iran's power plants, Donald Trump's sudden backdown is again fuelling TACO taunts.

items.title: World markets reverse course, stocks rally as Trump postpones Iran military strikes
Canberrans encouraged to consider alternate transport amid fuel price hike
items.description: The ACT transport minister says there's "never been a better time" to consider purchasing an electric vehicle or using alternative transport modes as high petrol prices bite.

items.title: Energy, water, bonds: What are Iran’s targets if Trump hits power plants?
items.description: Donald Trump's Strait of Hormuz deadline for Iran raises concerns about attacks on energy in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump can declare victory in Iran – and he should
items.description: The US president has proven that hitting hard and fast rather than engaging in endless negotiations guarantees victory.

items.title: ‘Substantial evidence’ of double-tap strike in killing of Gaza’s Hind Rajab
items.description: Avaaz analyses the timeline of events to conclude that violations show Israel systematically kills first responders.

items.title: Ilya Remeslo, formerly a pro-Putin blogger, has ended up in a Russian psychiatric hospital after criticizing the president. What happened?
items.description: After seemingly switching sides, a pro-government blogger in Russia who criticized President Vladimir Putin has been admitted to psychiatric care in St. Petersburg. Did he go rogue?

items.title: Mariana Da Cruz and Morenike on blending genres, identity and artificial intelligence
items.description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with Mariana Da Cruz from the band Da Cruz and rising star Morenike. Da Cruz make contemporary world music with a message, often about the political and social situation in Brazil. Their latest album "Son Sistema" is an exploration of Mariana's African and Brazilian roots through sound, and also looks at the future of Black club music. The genres range from South African amapiano to Brazilian baile funk and Caribbean shatta.

items.title: World experiences hottest 11 years on record, WMO warns
items.description: The world has just recorded its hottest 11-year period, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization.

items.title: Dr. Andrew Smith: Elections are won by 'constructing a vision that articulates political values'
items.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Dr. Andrew Smith, Historian of Modern France and Lecturer at Queen Mary University of London. What stands out to our guest is not just that the Socialists held Paris, but how they did so: by resisting opportunistic alliances and instead presenting a coherent, independent platform rooted in credibility and local recognition. According to Dr. Smith, the narrative of far-right inevitability is overstated. While they continue to make strategic inroads in small cities, these results demonstrate that their ascent is neither uniform nor guaranteed.

items.title: Why the oil and gas price shock from the Iran war won’t just fade away
items.description: Rerouting and diversification cannot help when a significant chunk of oil and gas have been taken off the market.

items.title: Saudi Arabia orders Iranian military attache, four embassy staff to leave
items.description: Saudi Arabia ‌has ⁠come under attack by ⁠hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones since the start of U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, the ​vast majority of ‌which have been intercepted, authorities said

items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran's Foreign Ministry denies talks with U.S., as Trump sees 'regime change'
items.description: Iran Foreign Ministry says got messages from 'friendly countries' about U.S. request for talks

items.title: 'Moby Dick' whales headbutting captured on video for first time
items.description: Historic accounts dating back two centuries detail ships rammed and sunk by sperm whales, now scientists claim to have filmed the species for the first time displaying headbutting behaviour.

items.title: Australia and European Union to sign free trade agreement decades in the making
items.description: An EU trade agreement almost a decade in the making looks set to be signed but some industry groups are not happy.

items.title: Thousands of Victorian teachers and support staff strike amid pay dispute
items.description: About 500 state schools could close or face significant disruptions today, as teachers walk off the job for the first time in 13 years, says the Australian Education Union.

items.title: Former Kenyan minister reappears after disappearance sparks alarm
items.description: Former Kenyan cabinet minister Raphael Tuju has said he went into hiding after suspecting he was being followed, a day after his disappearance prompted fears he may have been abducted.

items.title: Italian voters reject Giorgia Meloni's judicial reform in referendum defeat
items.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni conceded defeat on Monday in a referendum which sought to overhaul the country's judiciary. The vote is a major blow to Meloni's far-right leadership, and could weaken her re-election chances next year.

items.title: Hong Kong: New rule forces people to surrender passwords
items.description: The rule applies to investigations connected to the financial hub's strict national security law. Those who fail to comply face up to a year in prison or a fine of over $10,000.

items.title: France: Leftist Emmanuel Gregoire becomes new mayor of Paris
items.description: Gregoire replaces fellow socialist Anne Hidalgo as the French capital’s mayor. The local elections are being closely watched to gauge the political mood in the country ahead of next year’s presidential race.

items.title: Scores hurt after Iranian missiles hit Israeli desert towns
items.description: Uri Shacham, the chief of staff of Israel’s ambulance service, said at least eight buildings were damaged by the missile, ‌which left a crater not far from the apartment blocks

items.title: How the Iran war is about to hit your wallet
items.description: Strikes on gas sites in the Iran war are driving up energy costs, pushing up prices for power, food and more worldwide.

items.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
items.description: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

items.title: Slovenia's liberals and populists neck and neck, preliminary results show
items.description: Prime Minister Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement won 28.5% of the votes while the right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party, or SDS, led by ex-premier Janez Jansa — an admirer of U.S. President Donald Trump — had 28.1%

items.title: Trump U-turn on Hormuz could be due to 'pressure' from Gulf nations
items.description: President Donald Trump extended his deadline for Iran to reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. He had previously given a 48-deadline to Tehran, which would lead to strikes on Iranian power plants were the Strait not to reopen. The backtracking could have been encouraged by Gulf States, who have been targeted by vigorous strikes by Iran; FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.

items.title: Iran's energy sites still on Trump's target list
items.description: US President Trump has delayed strikes on Iran's power plants for five days, citing "productive" talks with Tehran. DW asks what infrastructure US forces could still bomb to force a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: President Trump and Iranian officials have conflicting stories about ongoing talks
items.description: President Trump is ordering the U.S. military to hold off on striking Iranian power plants, citing "productive" talks with Iran, but Iranian officials deny there's any dialogue with the U.S.

items.title: Kenyan ex-foreign minister arrested and accused of staging his disappearance
items.description: The reported disappearance of Raphael Tuju had led to claims he may have been abducted.

items.title: Russia's Novatek strikes a preliminary deal on LNG supply to Vietnam
Two pilots killed in runway collision at New York’s LaGuardia Airport
items.description: The Air Canada Express plane tilted back on its tail on the tarmac with a smashed cockpit and flanked by emergency vehicles with flashing lights

items.title: North Korea’s Kim reappointed as president of state affairs
Fuel costs forcing potential 'life and death' decisions for rural patients
items.description: The Rural Doctors Association says some patients may be forced to make "life and death" decisions due to the rising cost of fuel in regional Australia.

items.title: Dollar scarcity in Venezuela forces small firms to raise prices, turn to crypto
Path ahead for Nepal’s new leadership
items.description: After securing a historic mandate in Nepal’s polls, Balendra Shah and the RSP face the task of navigating the West Asia conflict and managing ties with India, China and the U.S. While capitalising on old-guard fatigue proved an effective electoral strategy, the new leadership now faces a test in international relations

items.title: What's next for Germany's troubled car giants?
items.description: German automakers are in a crisis due to stagnant sales, falling profits and constant strategy shifts. Can the industry recover?

items.title: Lionel Jospin: 'Highly-respected moral and intellectual figure among left wing'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Damien Lecomte, Political Scientist and Researcher at CRPS-CESSP, Paris Pantheon Sorbonne University. According to Dr. Lecomte, there is a persistent tension between unity and fragmentation in French politics, and the legacy of Lionel Jospin illustrates this paradox: a figure of moral integrity and intellectual rigor who successfully governed a broad left-wing coalition, yet he was ultimately defeated by a divided electorate on the left.

items.title: Survivors recall horror of dictatorship, 50 years on from coup
items.description: Survivors of Argentina's 1976–1983 dictatorship are representatives of a decimated generation whose stories of torture, death and exile are coming back to life, 50 years after the coup. Leer más

items.title: ‘Sahel is no longer fooled’ Niger civil society leader
items.description: Niger civil society leader Abdourahamane Oumarou has called for mass protests across Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger.

items.title: U.S. military pause on Iran’s attack: India closely monitors developments
items.description: The announcement led to cooling of oil prices with benchmark Brent Crude futures (May) slipping more than 12%

items.title: Ex-TC Narelle leaves NT with minimal damage but emergency 'not over yet'
items.description: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle has left the Northern Territory after dumping more than 200mm of rain on the Top End overnight, but authorities have warned the emergency is "not over".

items.title: Trials into crimes against humanity press on amid political tensions
items.description: Thousands investigated, hundreds convicted, but ageing suspects, slow courts and political pressure threaten progress. As the 50th anniversary of coup d’état draws nears, government criticism raises concerns over accountability for crimes against humanity. Leer más

items.title: UK PM Starmer says no threat from Iran on Britain, calls for de-escalation
items.description: UK PM Keir Starmer says routine assessments conclude that the UK is not a target of Iran amid global tensions.

items.title: Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever, WMO warns
items.description: The world's oceans have broken heat records for nine straight years, glaciers are retreating and extreme weather is killing thousands. The only way to avoid the worst is to urgently ditch fossil fuels.

items.title: Adorni under pressure as fresh complaint questions state contracts
items.description: New complaint from lawmaker alleges irregularities in public tenders linked to Tecnópolis exhibition centre and venue, adding to scrutiny over the Cabinet chief’s conduct and potential conflicts of interest involving his wife. Leer más

items.title: Argentina to now face Mauritania and Zambia in March friendlies
items.description: World champions will play both matches at La Bombonera after the Finalissima against Spain was cancelled and a planned friendly against Guatemala fell through, due to FIFA regulations. Leer más

items.title: The 24th of March
items.description: Over the years, I have learned that – for the most part – those who reach the Presidency and lead Argentina do not possess superior virtue. They are just as flawed as the average person. Leer más

items.title: Disillusioned voters back truth in political advertising reform
items.description: Civic groups are calling for sweeping reforms to restore falling trust, which experts have linked to a resurgence of populist "protest" voting at the expense of the major parties.

items.title: Elon Musk misled shareholders in effort to drive down Twitter stock price, jury finds
items.description: A federal jury in California found Friday that tech titan Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders in an effort to drive down the company's share price as he was poised to buy it in a $44 billion deal.

items.title: ASX falls, Asia tumbles, gold crashes as Trump keeps markets on edge
items.description: The Australian share market fell, Asian shares were generally clobbered and precious metals were a long way off earlier peaks. But it could have been worse.

items.title: Are your maths skills sharper than a Year 9 student's?
items.description: Aussie students have just been put to the test with NAPLAN. Now it's your turn to see how you'd go in their shoes.

items.title: Kenya Airways sees surge in demand as Middle East conflict disrupts global travel
items.description: Kenya Airways says bookings on its flights have surged sharply in recent weeks, as disruptions linked to the conflict in the Middle East reshape global travel patterns.

items.title: Italy is voting on whether to change its constitution. What does this mean for Meloni?
items.description: The dry subject matter of this weekend's vote seems obscure but for many it is a vote on the PM.

items.title: News24 | London Jews fear 'escalation' after attack on ambulances
items.description: In London’s Golders Green, with its large Jewish community, residents warned of growing antisemitism on Monday after an early morning arson attack on four volunteer ambulances run by a Jewish organisation.

items.title: MPs push gambling ad ban as study reveals targeting of women
items.description: Gambling is being promoted as fun, social and "harmless" for women through social media and lifestyle marketing, in a shift researchers warn is normalising betting and obscuring the risks.

items.title: ICE agents deployed in US airports to ease security lines amid shutdown
items.description: Agents from the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE, will be deployed to airports across the country on March 23 in an effort to ease long security lines. The move comes as the shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security stretches into its second month, causing major disruptions for travellers due to a lack of security staff.

items.title: Exclusive-Broadcasters urge EU to tighten rules for Big Tech in smart TV standoff
Turbulence in fuel markets hitting African airlines hard
items.description: Turbulence in the fuel markets caused by the US-Israel war on Iran is hitting African airlines hard. The continent imports 70 percent of its jet fuel and kerosene and fuel costs make up a greater share of operating costs than for other carriers.

items.title: Italy’s Meloni concedes referendum defeat, calling it ‘a lost opportunity’
items.description: The prime minister admits defeat in the high-stakes vote on a justice reform package but vows to keep her seat.

items.title: Alliance of Sahel states outraged at EU demand to free Niger's president
items.description: The European parliament voted last week to pass a resolution demanding the "immediate" release of Niger's former president Mohamed Bazoum, who was ousted in a coup in 2023 and has been detained ever since. The Alliance of Sahel states reacted with indignation.

items.title: 170 e-bike riders fined in blitz cracking down on 'silly, dangerous' behaviour
items.description: More than 300 e-bike riders have been stopped, including e-scooter riders, during an ongoing police operation in the Sutherland Shire.

items.title: 50 tales for 50 years: New book collects personal stories of dictatorship’s crimes
items.description: 'La Retaguardia,' media outlet specialising in coverage of trials probing crimes against humanity during the military dictatorship, has compiled accounts drawn from court files and testimonies given in court. Leer más

items.title: Using every drop: Physics answers a crucial kitchen question
items.description: How long does it take for the last drop of milk or oil to drip out of the bottle? US scientists have probed this everyday conundrum.

items.title: UK police probe anti-Semitic attack on Jewish ambulances
items.description: British police said they were investigating an online claim of responsibility for an arson attack after four volunteer ambulances run by a Jewish organisation based next to a London synagogue were set on fire early Monday. Police said the attack is being treated as an anti-Semitic hate crime.

items.title: Venezuela’s 600% inflation undercuts Trump’s boasts of revival

items.title: Perth mother struggles to get specialist care for her three-year-old son
items.description: A Perth mother whose three-year-old son has a complex form of epilepsy, believes the health system is failing her son because he is being pushed between two public hospitals.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: White House installs statue of Christopher Columbus on its grounds
items.description: Trump called Columbus “the original American hero and one of the most gallant and visionary men to ever walk the face of the Earth”

items.title: Germany has a shortage of workers
items.description: The European nation, struggling to find skilled staff, is giving jobs to young people from India.

items.title: Trump shares SNL skit mocking Starmer as he speaks to UK PM over Iran war

items.title: A new threat to global shipping as Yemen's Houthis threaten to close vital Bab el-Mandeb Strait
items.description: Yemen's Houthis are considering to block the Bab el-Mandeb Strait to ships from aggressor countries against members of the so-called "axis of resistance."

items.title: News24 | UK police investigating ‘antisemitic hate crime’ after four Jewish ambulances torched
items.description: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish community organisation in north London were set ablaze ‌overnight in a suspected antisemitic hate crime, police said.

items.title: Czechs plan major rally as MPs mull 'foreign agent' law
items.description: Prague could see one of its largest demonstrations in recent years on Saturday. Many Czechs are preparing to protest what organizers of the rally describe as democratic backsliding under Prime Minister Andrej Babis.

items.title: News24 | Iran rejects Trump deadline on Hormuz, threatens to strike ‘all energy infrastructure’
items.description: Iran warned it would strike energy and water infrastructure across the Gulf if US President Donald Trump follows through on his threat to attack its electricity grid, raising fears of mass disruption in a region heavily dependent on desalination for drinking water.

items.description: Defence lawyers for the son of Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit called for him to be acquitted of charges of raping four women.

items.title: Planet is storing more heat than it can release, UN warns
items.description: The amount of heat trapped by the Earth has reached record levels. According to the UN's annual State of the Climate report, published this Monday, the last 11 years have been the warmest ever recorded. For the first time, the World Meteorological Organization is warning of a growing "energy imbalance", driven by rising greenhouse gas emissions. Scientists also fear that a natural warming phase called El Niño could soon bring further heat records. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe explains.

items.title: Chad to deploy 800 troops to Haiti's Gang Suppression Force as Kenya steps back

items.title: Guinea cracks down on suspected GSIM networks

items.title: Jihadist prisoner release secures fuel convoy truce in Mali
items.description: More than 100 suspected jihadists have been freed in Mali under a deal to halt attacks on fuel convoys that had paralyzed the capital, Bamako, official and security sources told AFP on Sunday.

items.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
items.description: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.
